content.problemStatement:
CodeChef has received an order from the President of Mithai country for his son's birthday cake. The president is a person of very high temper and CodeChef doesn't want to tick him off, so he had to prepare a cake exactly as described by the President's son. He asked for a cake with **N** layers and each layer has to be of a type specified by him. The type of layer is represented by a lowercase letter from the English alphabet.

CodeChef asked his sous Chef, Jalebi Bai, to make this cake, who was very sleepy due to a very long and tiring journey to a planet far far away earlier. Due to tiredness, Jalebi Bai screwed up the layers while baking the cake. Thankfully, it has the same number of layers as required, but any of the layers may or may not be the same as described in the order. CodeChef is really worried because of this, as making a new cake will cost him a huge amount of money.

At this point of time, Samosa Bhai comes to the rescue. He has a layer swapper (patent pending) which can swap the layers of a cake without ruining the cake. This swapper has a limitation that it can swap layers separated **exactly** by distance **D** only, meaning there should be **exactly** **D-1** layers in between the two layers to be swapped.

You have to tell if the cake made by Jalebi Bai can be changed into the cake described by the President's son using Samosa Bhai's swapper.


Input Format
The first line contains **T**, the number of test cases.


The first line of each test case contains **N** and **D**.


The next line contains a lowercase string **A** representing the cake described by the President's son.


The next line contains a lowercase string **B** representing the cake made by Jalebi Bai.



Output Format
For each test case, output "**Yes**" [without quotes] if it is possible to transform the cake **B** into cake **A** using the layer swapper. Otherwise, output "**No**" [without quotes].
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 100000
A and B consist only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and D.
- The second line contains a string A of length N (desired cake).
- The third line contains a string B of length N (actual cake).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if B can be transformed into A using swaps of exactly distance D, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each test case is evaluated by comparing character counts inside each modulo‑D group. The first, second, and fourth cases satisfy the condition, while the third and fifth do not.

hidden.label: no swaps possible, exact match required

solution.approach: Two positions can be swapped only if their indices differ by exactly D. Repeating such swaps allows any permutation inside each residue class modulo D, because we can swap i with i+D, i+D with i+2D, etc., effectively performing adjacent swaps within the class. Therefore, the transformation is possible iff for every residue r (0 ≤ r < D) the multiset of characters of A at positions i where i % D = r equals the multiset of characters of B at the same positions. Implementation: for each test case create an array cnt[ D ][26 ] initialized to zero. Scan the strings; for each index i increase cnt[i % D][ A[i] - 'a' ] and decrease cnt[i % D][ B[i] - 'a' ]. After processing, if all counters are zero the answer is "Yes", otherwise "No". The algorithm runs in O(N·alphabet) time and O(D·alphabet) memory, both well within limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (alphabet size 26 is constant).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(D·26) integers, at most O(N) in the worst case when D ≈ N.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); D = int(next(it))
        A = next(it).strip()
        B = next(it).strip()
        cnt = [[0]*26 for _ in range(D)]
        for i in range(N):
            r = i % D
            cnt[r][ord(A[i]) - 97] += 1
            cnt[r][ord(B[i]) - 97] -= 1
        ok = all(all(v==0 for v in row) for row in cnt)
        out_lines.append('Yes' if ok else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
